source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure 'Unattached disks' are encrypted with customer managed keys

input_row.source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure 'Unattached disks' are encrypted with customer managed keys
input_row.description: By default, IBM Cloud Block Storage provides provider-managed encryption for all data. For enhanced security, customers can bring their own encryption keys and manage them through IBM Key Management Services – Key Protect or Hyper Protect Crypto Services (HPCS). Provider-managed encryption is turned on by default and cannot be turned off.
input_row.rationale: With customer-managed keys, only customers can access and manage their master root keys, giving them full control over their data security.
input_row.audit: You can use the IBM Cloud Block Storage configuration properties to verify whether a Cloud Object Storage bucket is enabled to use Key Protect or Hyper Protect key management services. From Console, Navigate to your Cloud Object Storage instance: 1. In the IBM Cloud console, go to Menu icon > VPC Infrastructure > Storage > Block storage volumes. A list of all block storage volumes displays. 2. For each volumes listed without any value in the "Attachment Type" column, ensure the encryption field is Not pointing to "Provider Managed".
input_row.remediation: You will not be able to change encryption option once data is already written to a Cloud Block Storage Volume. In order to ensure that objects are encrypted using customer managed keys you will need to create a new Cloud Block Storage volume, set it to use either Key Protect or Hyper Protect key management service and then upload/copy the existing objects to this new volume. Default Value: Provider managed References: 1. https://cloud.ibm.com/vpc-ext

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control can be audited through the IBM Cloud APIs and IBM Cloud CLI. The IBM Cloud Block Storage volumes can be queried to check the encryption type associated with unattached disks. Given the description and audit instructions, the main requirement is verifying the encryption of unattached volumes and ensuring they're using customer-managed keys rather than provider-managed encryption. This verification step can be automated through the IBM Cloud API and CLI commands, which are capable of retrieving block storage volume details, including their encryption settings.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_vpc_block_storage_unattached_disks_customer_managed_encryption

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **IBM Cloud API/CLI Calls**:
  - Use `ibmcloud is volumes` command to list all block storage volumes along with their properties.
  - Filter volumes based on the "Attachment Type" to identify unattached disks.
  - Check the "Encryption" property for each unattached volume.
- **Configuration Verification**:
  - Ensure that the "Encryption" field does not state "Provider Managed".
  - Instead, it should reference "Key Protect" or "Hyper Protect Crypto Services".
- **Pass/Fail Criteria**:
  - **Pass**: All unattached volumes must use customer-managed encryption (Key Protect or Hyper Protect keys).
  - **Fail**: Any unattached volume using provider-managed encryption.
- **Resource Enumeration**:
  - Enumerate across all block storage volumes within the specified account/region to ensure compliance.
- **Expected Values**:
  - The expected configuration should explicitly indicate the use of customer-managed keys.

In summary, the audit approach leverages IBM Cloud API capabilities to automatically verify encryption settings of unattached IBM Cloud Block Storage volumes, ensuring compliance with the CIS benchmark by confirming the use of customer-managed keys.
